mission: Derive the strongest theorem-grade neutral source carrier available inside the current fermionic/CAR IO extension.

theorem_1_even_car_neutral_carrier.status: derived / scoped within the existing fermionic/CAR extension
theorem_1_even_car_neutral_carrier.statement: The existing lowest-shell fermionic bridge already contains a neutral composite carrier: the gauge-neutral even CAR subalgebra generated by quadratic bilinears. In particular, the scalar density operator N = sum_i a_i^dagger a_i is an SO(3)-scalar, gauge-neutral, bosonic composite observable on the admitted fermionic carrier.
theorem_1_even_car_neutral_carrier.exact_object: A_neutral^(2) = Alg{ a_i^dagger a_j, a_i a_j, a_i^dagger a_j^dagger }_even

theorem_2_support_compression_scale.assumption: the neutral composite carrier is realized as a relativistically compressed fermionic many-body sector on the support branch
theorem_2_support_compression_scale.statement: Under the compression assumption, the neutral composite carrier acquires a unique support-branch energy scale E_F(R) determined by count and radius alone.

theorem_3_full_count_crossing.statement: If the neutral composite carrier inherits the full puncture count, its compression scale crosses the already-fixed baryogenesis target T_f,target at the unique radius R_eta reported here.

theorem_4_source_carrier_upgrade.statement: The previous internal statement 'a new high-energy neutral source carrier theorem is required' can now be tightened. A neutral source carrier exists already as a composite even-CAR sector. What remains missing is not neutrality, but source transfer and freeze-out structure.
still_open: a visible B-L transfer or decay theorem
a freeze-out/rate theorem selecting the active crossing
a theorem relating the neutral composite carrier to the visible baryon sector
